questions.stem: I ______ the book last week, but I haven't finished reading it yet.
questions.explanation: 选A。last week是明确的过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时；bought是瞬间动词，不能与现在完成时连用表示持续状态。B选项have bought虽然强调对现在的影响，但last week这一具体过去时间状语禁止与现在完成时连用；C是一般现在时，不符合时间背景；D是过去完成时，需要另一个过去动作作为参照点，此处没有。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is playing basketball over there is my new classmate.
questions.explanation: 选A。先行词the boy指人，定语从句中缺少主语，故用关系代词who。B选项which用于指物；C选项whom用于指人但在从句中作宾语；D选项what不能引导定语从句。口诀：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that，缺主语用who/which，缺宾语用whom/which/可省略。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The letter ______ in English yesterday morning.
questions.explanation: 选C。yesterday表明是过去时，letter与write是被动关系（信被写），故用一般过去时的被动语态was written。A是一般现在时主动；B是一般过去时主动；D是一般现在时被动，时态不符。被动语态结构：be + 过去分词，时态看be动词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Look at the dark clouds in the sky. It ______ rain heavily soon.
questions.explanation: 选A。must表肯定推测，意为'一定、肯定会'，根据乌云密布的迹象，下雨的可能性极大。B选项might表示'可能'，但可能性较小（约30%），与乌云密布的强证据不符；C选项can't表示'不可能'，与语境相反；D选项shouldn't表示'不应该'，不用于天气推测。口诀：must肯定，can't否定，might不确定。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We need to ______ a good solution to this difficult problem before Friday.
questions.explanation: 选C。come up with意为'想出、提出（主意、计划、解决方案等）'，与solution搭配恰当。A选项look for强调寻找的动作，不强调结果；B选项find out意为'查明、弄清楚（真相、事实）'；D选项get on意为'上车'或'进展'。固定搭配：come up with an idea/solution/plan。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It's getting cold outside. Please ______ your coat before you go out.
questions.explanation: 选A。put on意为'穿上'，与coat搭配表示穿衣服。B选项put off意为'推迟'；C选项put up意为'张贴、搭建、举起'；D选项put away意为'把...收起来、放好'。put短语辨析：on穿戴，off取消/推迟，up举起/张贴，away收起。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The weather forecast says it will be ______ tomorrow. Don't forget to take an umbrella.
questions.explanation: 选B。rainy是形容词，意为'下雨的'，由名词rain加后缀-y构成。A选项sunny（晴朗的）与后文带伞矛盾；C选项rainless（无雨的）由rain+less（无...的）构成，与语境相反；D选项rainful不是标准英语词汇。后缀-y加在名词后构成形容词：cloud→cloudy, sun→sunny, rain→rainy。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 选B。根据前文'many students didn't take it seriously'（不认真对待）和'just another'（只是另一个...），可推断学生认为这是'boring（无聊的）'活动。A、D选项为积极词汇，与'didn't take it seriously'态度不符；C选项important与just another（暗含'老一套、没什么特别'）语气矛盾。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students realize the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought it was just another (8)___ school activity. However, everything changed when our science teacher, Mr. Zhang, showed us some shocking photos of polluted rivers near our city. We could see dead fish and dirty water. It really opened our (9)___.

After that, students became very active. My classmates and I decided to work on waste sorting. We made colorful posters and (10)___ them up around the school. We also set up special bins for different kinds of rubbish. To our surprise, most students started to use them correctly within a week.

The project has taught us that small actions can make a big difference. Now, our school is much (11)___ than before. I feel proud to be part of this (12)___.

questions.explanation: 选A。固定搭配open one's eyes意为'使某人大开眼界、使认清事实'，符合语境'看到污染照片后醒悟'。B选项ears（耳朵）常与open搭配表示'侧耳倾听'，但此处是视觉体验；C选项hearts（心）常与open搭配表示'敞开心扉'；D选项mouths（嘴）无此搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students realize the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought it was just another (8)___ school activity. However, everything changed when our science teacher, Mr. Zhang, showed us some shocking photos of polluted rivers near our city. We could see dead fish and dirty water. It really opened our (9)___.

After that, students became very active. My classmates and I decided to work on waste sorting. We made colorful posters and (10)___ them up around the school. We also set up special bins for different kinds of rubbish. To our surprise, most students started to use them correctly within a week.

The project has taught us that small actions can make a big difference. Now, our school is much (11)___ than before. I feel proud to be part of this (12)___.

questions.explanation: 选A。固定搭配put up意为'张贴'，与posters（海报）搭配恰当。B选项took up意为'开始从事、占据（时间/空间）'；C选项gave up意为'放弃'；D选项turned up意为'调大（音量）、出现'。put up有'张贴、搭建、举起'等含义，是中考高频短语。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students realize the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought it was just another (8)___ school activity. However, everything changed when our science teacher, Mr. Zhang, showed us some shocking photos of polluted rivers near our city. We could see dead fish and dirty water. It really opened our (9)___.

After that, students became very active. My classmates and I decided to work on waste sorting. We made colorful posters and (10)___ them up around the school. We also set up special bins for different kinds of rubbish. To our surprise, most students started to use them correctly within a week.

The project has taught us that small actions can make a big difference. Now, our school is much (11)___ than before. I feel proud to be part of this (12)___.

questions.explanation: 选B。than是比较级标志词，much修饰比较级加强程度，故用cleaner（更干净的）。A是原级；C、D是最高级，用于三者及以上比较。比较级常见标志词：than, much, even, far, a lot, a little等。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students realize the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought it was just another (8)___ school activity. However, everything changed when our science teacher, Mr. Zhang, showed us some shocking photos of polluted rivers near our city. We could see dead fish and dirty water. It really opened our (9)___.

After that, students became very active. My classmates and I decided to work on waste sorting. We made colorful posters and (10)___ them up around the school. We also set up special bins for different kinds of rubbish. To our surprise, most students started to use them correctly within a week.

The project has taught us that small actions can make a big difference. Now, our school is much (11)___ than before. I feel proud to be part of this (12)___.

questions.explanation: 选A。前文提到'our school is much cleaner than before'（学校比以前干净），说明发生了'change（变化）'，作者为参与这一改变而自豪。B选项lesson（课程、教训）虽可搭配，但不如change贴合全文'校园变绿、环境改善'的主题；C选项game（游戏）与文意不符；D选项problem（问题）与proud（自豪）情感色彩矛盾。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students realize the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought it was just another (8)___ school activity. However, everything changed when our science teacher, Mr. Zhang, showed us some shocking photos of polluted rivers near our city. We could see dead fish and dirty water. It really opened our (9)___.

After that, students became very active. My classmates and I decided to work on waste sorting. We made colorful posters and (10)___ them up around the school. We also set up special bins for different kinds of rubbish. To our surprise, most students started to use them correctly within a week.

The project has taught us that small actions can make a big difference. Now, our school is much (11)___ than before. I feel proud to be part of this (12)___.

questions.stem: What did the students put the third piece of apple into?
questions.explanation: 选B。根据第三段'Then we treated the other three pieces differently. We put the second piece into plain water, the third into lemon juice...'可知，第三片苹果放入了柠檬汁中。细节题解题技巧：找准题干关键词（third piece），快速定位原文对应句，避免被其他干扰项（如第二片放清水、第四片放盐水的信息）迷惑。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever wondered why apples turn brown after you cut them? Last week, our biology teacher showed us the answer through a simple but interesting experiment.

First, we cut an apple into four pieces. We left the first piece on the table as the "control group". Then we treated the other three pieces differently. We put the second piece into plain water, the third into lemon juice, and the fourth into salt water. After 30 minutes, we checked the results.

The piece on the table had turned completely brown. The one in plain water was slightly brown. Surprisingly, the piece in lemon juice stayed almost white and fresh-looking. The salt water piece was less brown than the control but darker than the lemon juice one.

Our teacher explained that when apples are cut, they meet oxygen in the air. This causes a chemical reaction called oxidation (氧化). Lemon juice contains vitamin C, which can slow down this reaction. That's why many people put lemon juice on cut fruits to keep them fresh.

This experiment made us understand that science is not just in textbooks but also in our daily lives. By observing carefully and asking questions, we can discover many interesting secrets around us.

questions.stem: The underlined word 'oxidation' in Paragraph 4 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 选A。根据上下文线索：1）前文提到'apples turn brown'（苹果变褐）；2）'meet oxygen in the air'（接触空气中的氧气）；3）'chemical reaction'（化学反应）；4）后文提到lemon juice can slow down this reaction（柠檬汁能减缓这一反应）。结合化学常识，水果切开接触氧气变褐是氧化反应。词义猜测题技巧：关注同位语、定语从句、举例、因果关系等线索，利用构词法（oxygen氧气→oxide氧化物→oxidation氧化）。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever wondered why apples turn brown after you cut them? Last week, our biology teacher showed us the answer through a simple but interesting experiment.

First, we cut an apple into four pieces. We left the first piece on the table as the "control group". Then we treated the other three pieces differently. We put the second piece into plain water, the third into lemon juice, and the fourth into salt water. After 30 minutes, we checked the results.

The piece on the table had turned completely brown. The one in plain water was slightly brown. Surprisingly, the piece in lemon juice stayed almost white and fresh-looking. The salt water piece was less brown than the control but darker than the lemon juice one.

Our teacher explained that when apples are cut, they meet oxygen in the air. This causes a chemical reaction called oxidation (氧化). Lemon juice contains vitamin C, which can slow down this reaction. That's why many people put lemon juice on cut fruits to keep them fresh.

This experiment made us understand that science is not just in textbooks but also in our daily lives. By observing carefully and asking questions, we can discover many interesting secrets around us.

questions.stem: Why did the teacher do this experiment according to the passage?
options: A. To teach students how to cut apples skillfully
B. To show students that science is around us in daily life
C. To tell students to eat more lemons for vitamin C
D. To test which liquid is the best drink for people
questions.explanation: 选B。根据最后一段'This experiment made us understand that science is not just in textbooks but also in our daily lives'（这个实验让我们明白科学不仅在课本中，也在我们的日常生活中），可推断老师做实验的目的是展示科学就在身边。A选项'如何切苹果'只是实验手段而非目的；C选项'多吃柠檬'偏离主题；D选项'测试哪种饮料最好'曲解文意。推理题技巧：区分事实与观点，关注作者评价性语言（如made us understand that...）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever wondered why apples turn brown after you cut them? Last week, our biology teacher showed us the answer through a simple but interesting experiment.

First, we cut an apple into four pieces. We left the first piece on the table as the "control group". Then we treated the other three pieces differently. We put the second piece into plain water, the third into lemon juice, and the fourth into salt water. After 30 minutes, we checked the results.

The piece on the table had turned completely brown. The one in plain water was slightly brown. Surprisingly, the piece in lemon juice stayed almost white and fresh-looking. The salt water piece was less brown than the control but darker than the lemon juice one.

Our teacher explained that when apples are cut, they meet oxygen in the air. This causes a chemical reaction called oxidation (氧化). Lemon juice contains vitamin C, which can slow down this reaction. That's why many people put lemon juice on cut fruits to keep them fresh.

This experiment made us understand that science is not just in textbooks but also in our daily lives. By observing carefully and asking questions, we can discover many interesting secrets around us.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Cut an Apple Properly
B. The History of Apples in China
D. Why We Should Eat More Fresh Apples
questions.explanation: 选C。全文围绕课堂上做的'苹果氧化对比实验'展开，从实验步骤、现象观察到原理分析，因此'An Interesting Science Experiment'最能概括主旨。A选项'如何切苹果'只是实验细节；B选项'苹果历史'文中未提；D选项'为什么应多吃苹果'偏离主题。主旨题技巧：寻找高频词（experiment出现多次），排除以偏概全和偏离主题的选项，标题应涵盖全文核心内容。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever wondered why apples turn brown after you cut them? Last week, our biology teacher showed us the answer through a simple but interesting experiment.

First, we cut an apple into four pieces. We left the first piece on the table as the "control group". Then we treated the other three pieces differently. We put the second piece into plain water, the third into lemon juice, and the fourth into salt water. After 30 minutes, we checked the results.

The piece on the table had turned completely brown. The one in plain water was slightly brown. Surprisingly, the piece in lemon juice stayed almost white and fresh-looking. The salt water piece was less brown than the control but darker than the lemon juice one.

Our teacher explained that when apples are cut, they meet oxygen in the air. This causes a chemical reaction called oxidation (氧化). Lemon juice contains vitamin C, which can slow down this reaction. That's why many people put lemon juice on cut fruits to keep them fresh.

This experiment made us understand that science is not just in textbooks but also in our daily lives. By observing carefully and asking questions, we can discover many interesting secrets around us.

options: A. You can review a few English words or read a book instead.
B. However, having hobbies is actually good for your study.
C. But this problem can be solved with good time management.
D. Put the hardest tasks on days when you have less homework.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 选C。前文提出'难以平衡学习与爱好'的问题，后文紧接着'Here are some useful tips'（以下是一些实用建议），因此此处需要承上启下的过渡句，C选项'但这个问题可以通过良好的时间管理得到解决'既回应了前文的problem，又引出下文的解决方案。A是具体建议，应在提出方法后使用；B转折太突兀；D是具体计划建议；E与文章主旨（平衡而非放弃）相悖。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students have trouble balancing study and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they can't finish homework but want to play basketball or paint. (17)___ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan every Sunday evening. Write down all the important tasks for the week. (18)___ This way, you won't forget anything important.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you have 15 minutes before dinner, don't just watch short videos. (19)___ Small pieces of time can add up to big progress.

(20)___ Hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and bring you joy. When you feel happy, you can study better.

Remember, balance is the key to a healthy school life.

options: A. You can review a few English words or read a book instead.
B. However, having hobbies is actually good for your study.
C. But this problem can be solved with good time management.
D. Put the hardest tasks on days when you have less homework.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 选D。前文提到'Write down all the important tasks'（写下所有重要任务），D选项'把最难的任务放在作业较少的日子'是对'制定计划'的具体展开，符合逻辑递进关系。A是碎片时间利用建议，应在第二点使用；B、C与上下文话题不符；E观点错误。选句子填空技巧：关注指代关系、逻辑连接词（first, second, however, therefore等）和词汇复现。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

First, make a plan every Sunday evening. Write down all the important tasks for the week. (18)___ This way, you won't forget anything important.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you have 15 minutes before dinner, don't just watch short videos. (19)___ Small pieces of time can add up to big progress.

(20)___ Hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and bring you joy. When you feel happy, you can study better.

Remember, balance is the key to a healthy school life.

options: A. You can review a few English words or read a book instead.
B. However, having hobbies is actually good for your study.
C. But this problem can be solved with good time management.
D. Put the hardest tasks on days when you have less homework.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 选A。前文说'不要只看短视频'，A选项'你可以改为复习几个英语单词或读一本书'是具体的替代建议，与'利用碎片时间'的主题一致。B、C、D与'15分钟碎片时间'的语境不符；E观点错误。注意instead一词表示替代前文提到的'watch short videos'。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students have trouble balancing study and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they can't finish homework but want to play basketball or paint. (17)___ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan every Sunday evening. Write down all the important tasks for the week. (18)___ This way, you won't forget anything important.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you have 15 minutes before dinner, don't just watch short videos. (19)___ Small pieces of time can add up to big progress.

(20)___ Hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and bring you joy. When you feel happy, you can study better.

Remember, balance is the key to a healthy school life.

options: A. You can review a few English words or read a book instead.
B. However, having hobbies is actually good for your study.
C. But this problem can be solved with good time management.
D. Put the hardest tasks on days when you have less homework.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 选B。后文说'Hobbies are not a waste of time'（爱好不是浪费时间），B选项'然而，拥有爱好实际上对你的学习有好处'作为段落主题句，引出对爱好价值的论述。However表示转折，针对前文可能暗示的'要利用所有时间学习'进行修正。A、C、D与'hobbies'主题不符；E与后文'not a waste of time'直接矛盾。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students have trouble balancing study and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they can't finish homework but want to play basketball or paint. (17)___ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan every Sunday evening. Write down all the important tasks for the week. (18)___ This way, you won't forget anything important.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you have 15 minutes before dinner, don't just watch short videos. (19)___ Small pieces of time can add up to big progress.

(20)___ Hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and bring you joy. When you feel happy, you can study better.

Remember, balance is the key to a healthy school life.
